note: DedupeCorners (lines 55-64) hand-rolls O(n^2) tolerance dedup via acc.Exists+DistanceTo. Replace with native Point3d.CullDuplicates(values, tolerance) which is O(n log n) sort-cull and BCL-tested. Also reusable across SpatialIndex.ValidatePoints and any other point dedup site.

ExtractCardinals + ExtremeAlongDirection + Choose + Dot (lines 47-86) implement world-axis extremum picking by hand. Curve.ExtremeParameters already returns the parameters needed; the dot-product comparison + manual aggregate fold is unnecessary. Use Point3d.SortAndCullPointList on the produced extrema points to dedupe planar collapses (currently a manual planar branch on line 60).

ValidatePoints (lines 151-185) materializes a Span<Point3d> -> array -> Enumerable.Range -> Aggregate fold to build a validated Point3d[]. The validation is just IsValid filtering; CullDuplicates already returns Point3d[] and respects tolerance. A simple LINQ filter over points.ToArray().Where(p=>p.IsValid).ToArray() is the correct shape; the 35-line aggregate is reinvented validation.

SpatialIndex.PointCloud exists (lines 23-32) but no public boundary surface (BridgeOutput) consumes it; the only Point3d entrypoints are Points/Bounds/MeshFaces/KNearest/Closest. Either expose a Cloud-typed BridgeOutput or remove the PointCloud factory. The RTree.CreatePointCloudTree wiring is correct; this is a missing-consumer issue.

Dot(point, direction) (lines 85-86) hand-implements scalar product. Vector3d * Vector3d operator overload exists for native dot, and Point3d also exposes operator-* (cast). Use `(Vector3d)point * direction` or `point.X*direction.X + ...` becomes Vector3d.Multiply. Trivial reduction but increases vocabulary alignment; pairs with W1A-002 elimination.

World-cardinal extrema for a planar/non-planar curve are essentially the bounding-box ZMin/ZMax/XMin etc. After IsPlanar projection, BoundingBox.GetCorners returns the canonical 8 (or 4) extrema directly. The current ExtremeAlongDirection iterates ExtremeParameters and recomputes positions; for a closed curve this is a tax over a single GetBoundingBox(true) + GetCorners() call. Combine with W1A-002 to delete ~40 lines.

MeshEdgeMidpoints uses Enumerable.Range(0, validated.TopologyEdges.Count).Select(i => validated.TopologyEdges.EdgeLine(i).PointAt(0.5)). MeshTopologyEdgeList exposes per-index helpers and an enumerator; using a foreach (banned) or LINQ over indices is fine, but the same pattern is duplicated for Topology.Adjacency/NonManifold (Extract.cs lines 390-447) — extract a single TopologyEdge enumerator and reuse. Architectural compression, not API discovery.
Already correctly used at Extract.cs:331 (mesh.Vertices.ToPoint3dArray()). No action; included as a positive-control finding to confirm sweep accuracy.

SpatialIndex.Search accepts BoundingBox or Sphere as the spatial probe shape. RTree exposes additional Search overloads for Sphere with a callback that fires per-node, which is identical to the pattern in use. No correction needed; flagged for the parallel-search dimension — RTree.SearchOverlaps is already called via Overlaps. Consumer-API gap: there is no boundary component yet that calls Overlaps.

Intersection.MeshLineSorted is the only Mesh-Line variant wired. Native API exposes LineLine/PlanePlane/PlaneCircle/PlaneSphere/CurveSelf — none used. Each is a simple primitive-pair intersect that fits the existing PairEvents/PairCurvePoint scaffold; absence of these branches caps the matcher's polymorphic surface artificially. Add cases when consumer demand arises.

Mass dispatch (Query.cs LengthMass/AreaMass/VolumeMass) correctly funnels through *MassProperties.Compute and disposes via DisposeAndProject. No action; positive-control finding to confirm the mass primitives are correctly threaded. Note the Brep overload of Compute uses (relativeTolerance, absoluteTolerance) order; verified at Query.cs:447-455 — correct.

FrameAtCentroid (lines 630-652) builds a Plane manually when OrientationIsReversed by negating YAxis. BrepFace.NormalAt(u,v) already returns the outward-corrected normal; constructing a Plane via (origin, xDirection, normal) gives the same orientation correction with no manual sign flip. Reduction is small but eliminates a coordinate-system invariant that needs maintaining.

SpatialIndex.DisposeTree (lines 142-145) is a one-line indirection wrapping tree.Dispose(); the Dispose method (137-141) uses a switch on a bool field. Replace with a single guard: `if (!disposed) { tree.Dispose(); disposed = true; }` would be the imperative form, but the LangExt-compliant form is Atom<bool> for one-shot — or simply `disposed = disposed ? true : DisposeTree();` collapsed. The DisposeTree helper has no purpose beyond the assignment trick.

SearchState (lines 219-261) is a private mutable buffer pattern with int[] / SpatialPair[] arrays + AddHit/AddPair counters. The doctrine is `[NEVER] Use mutable accumulation for domain transforms; use immutable folds`. Use `using System.Collections.Concurrent;` ConcurrentBag is BCL but the LanguageExt path is `Seq<T>` accumulated through the EventHandler closure (capturing an `Atom<Seq<int>>`). This is the largest pure-functional violation in the file.

BOUNDARY ADAPTER comment on line 79-80 acknowledges throw-on-fail; this is correct because Millimeters is a known-valid UnitSystem. Confirmed via api-catalog: Rhino.UnitSystem enum has Millimeters as a valid member. No action.

Probed candidates Mesh.CullDegenerateFaces / CombineIdentical / WeldUsingNgons returned ABSENT in the on-disk Rhino9/WIP RhinoCommon.dll. These were guessed convenience APIs that do NOT exist in this build — Rhino.Geometry.Mesh exposes CullDegenerateFaces? No. Mesh hygiene must go via Mesh.Faces.CullDegenerateFaces() (the collection-side helper) and Mesh.Weld (welding by angle). Update audit assumption: do not propose CullDegenerateFaces/CombineIdentical/WeldUsingNgons as additions; use the collection-side methods instead.
CurveFrame correctly threads PerpendicularFrameAt vs FrameAt via a perpendicular flag (line 507-518). Positive control: the dispatch is polymorphic and uses the right APIs.
IntersectionOutput / IntersectionKinds correctly project IntersectionEvent into IntersectionKind via IsPoint/IsOverlap (lines 268-307). Positive control.

FrameAtCentroid uses AreaMassProperties.Compute -> Centroid -> ClosestPoint -> FrameAt — three calls to land at a frame on the centroid. BrepFace.PointClosestTo + BrepFace.NormalAt is the same shape with the orientation-correction baked in. Combine with W1A-012; together they would replace the whole 22-line FrameAtCentroid with ~6 lines.

Topology.Adjacency / NonManifold cases (Extract.cs:386-450) all spell `Enumerable.Range(0, brep.Edges.Count).Where/Select`. BrepEdgeList implements IEnumerable<BrepEdge> so a direct `brep.Edges.Select((edge,i) => ...)` halves the line count. Same applies to the four MeshTopologyEdges branches.
